content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

September 10, 2010

Dan Dalager
Mayor, City of Encinitas
554 Hermes
Encinitas, Ca. 92024

RE: Your Request for Assistance
Our File No. W-10-145

Dear Mr. Dalager:

This letter is in response to your request for advice, on behalf of yourself regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); Regulation 18329(a), copy enclosed.) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. Your current question involves an analysis of actions already taken and a governmental decision that has already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the important, effective administration of the Act so mandated by Section 83111. Moreover, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Sukhi K. Brar
Intern, Legal Division

SKB:jgl

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

Your current question involves an analysis of actions already taken and a governmental decision that has already occurred. Issuing advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act as mandated by Section 83111. Moreover, the Commission must decline to give advice at this time, as the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor (Mayor Dan Dalager) sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act related to actions already taken and a governmental decision that had already occurred.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the question involves analysis of past conduct and an already-completed governmental decision, and because the same or similar issues are under investigation by the Enforcement Division.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (File No. W-10-145). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present, but the substantive legal reasoning and declination are clearly stated in the body. The document explicitly references Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F) as the basis for declining to provide advice.
